name: planted-defects-recall
ground_truth_note: Synthetic-but-grounded plan fixture (ACR-1.plan.md) against real lib/generation at b460257. 7 planted defects: (1) Phase 2 imports buildClampAuditEvent but Phase 1 exports buildClampAuditEntry (cross-phase mismatch, blocker — repo's #1 failure class); (2) import from @/lib/generation/telemetry/emit which does not exist at HEAD (blocker); (3) Phase 3 imports getMaxAllowedLoad but load-cap.ts exports getMaxAllowedLoadKg, and the fence's anchor/identifiers contradict validation.ts at HEAD (blocker cluster); (4) Phase 1 re-derives LOAD_EPSILON_KG which load-cap.ts:11-13 explicitly keeps unexported (suggestion/concern); (5) Phase 2 Oracle 'returns a well-formed audit entry' — exact values derivable from the fence (concern, test-contract); (6) Phase 3 TDD-exempt 'wiring only' on a behavior-adding fence (concern, test-contract); (7) Phase 2 catch {} swallows telemetry errors (concern, code-quality). KNOWN UNPLANTED-BUT-REAL fixture defects a strong run may also report (validated against iteration-1 baseline, 2026-07-08): new Date() breaks lib/generation's documented determinism invariant (CLAUDE.md + determinism-guard test); contract test paths co-located under lib/ vs the repo's tests/unit/generation mirror; 'npm run check' is biome not typecheck; module-level entries[] singleton leaks across runs; duplicate loadExceeds import; validate side never caps at HEAD (premise gap); isMeaningfulClamp unused export (knip). These count toward the row ceiling but are legitimate — do not penalize them semantically.
